```
import type {
  EditorConfig,
  LexicalEditor,
  LexicalNode,
  NodeKey,
  SerializedLexicalNode,
  Spread,
} from 'lexical'
import { $applyNodeReplacement, DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
import type { ReactElement } from 'react'

import { FootnoteSectionRenderer } from '../components/renderers/FootnoteSectionRenderer'
import { createRendererDecoration } from '../components/RendererWrapper'

export type SerializedFootnoteSectionNode = Spread<
  {
    definitions: Record<string, string>
  },
  SerializedLexicalNode
>

export class FootnoteSectionNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
  __definitions: Record<string, string>

  static getType(): string {
    return 'footnote-section'
  }

  static clone(node: FootnoteSectionNode): FootnoteSectionNode {
    return new FootnoteSectionNode({ ...node.__definitions }, node.__key)
  }

  constructor(definitions: Record<string, string>, key?: NodeKey) {
    super(key)
    this.__definitions = definitions
  }

  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    const div = document.createElement('div')
    div.className = 'rich-footnote-section'
    return div
  }

  updateDOM(): boolean {
    return false
  }

  isInline(): boolean {
    return false
  }

  static importJSON(
    serializedNode: SerializedFootnoteSectionNode,
  ): FootnoteSectionNode {
    return $createFootnoteSectionNode(serializedNode.definitions)
  }

  exportJSON(): SerializedFootnoteSectionNode {
    return {
      ...super.exportJSON(),
      type: 'footnote-section',
      definitions: this.__definitions,
      version: 1,
    }
  }

  getDefinitions(): Record<string, string> {
    return this.getLatest().__definitions
  }

  setDefinitions(definitions: Record<string, string>): void {
    const writable = this.getWritable()
    writable.__definitions = definitions
  }

  getDefinition(identifier: string): string | undefined {
    return this.getLatest().__definitions[identifier]
  }

  setDefinition(identifier: string, content: string): void {
    const writable = this.getWritable()
    writable.__definitions = {
      ...writable.__definitions,
      [identifier]: content,
    }
  }

  removeDefinition(identifier: string): void {
    const writable = this.getWritable()
    const { [identifier]: _, ...rest } = writable.__definitions
    writable.__definitions = rest
  }

  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    return createRendererDecoration(
      'FootnoteSection',
      FootnoteSectionRenderer,
      {
        definitions: this.__definitions,
        nodeKey: this.__key,
      },
    )
  }
}

export function $createFootnoteSectionNode(
  definitions: Record<string, string> = {},
): FootnoteSectionNode {
  return $applyNodeReplacement(new FootnoteSectionNode(definitions))
}

export function $isFootnoteSectionNode(
  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
): node is FootnoteSectionNode {
  return node instanceof FootnoteSectionNode
}
```

```
import { PortalThemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
import { CheckListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalCheckListPlugin'
import { LexicalComposer } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposer'
import { LexicalErrorBoundary } from '@lexical/react/LexicalErrorBoundary'
import { HistoryPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalHistoryPlugin'
import { LinkPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalLinkPlugin'
import { ListPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalListPlugin'
import { RichTextPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalRichTextPlugin'
import { TabIndentationPlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalTabIndentationPlugin'
import { TablePlugin } from '@lexical/react/LexicalTablePlugin'

import { allEditNodes } from '../config-edit'
import { ColorSchemeProvider } from '../context/ColorSchemeContext'
import { RendererConfigProvider } from '../context/RendererConfigContext'
import { setResolvedEditNodes } from '../node-registry'
import { AlertPlugin } from '../plugins/AlertPlugin'
import { AutoFocusPlugin } from '../plugins/AutoFocusPlugin'
import { AutoLinkPlugin } from '../plugins/AutoLinkPlugin'
import { EditorRefPlugin } from '../plugins/EditorRefPlugin'
import { FootnotePlugin } from '../plugins/FootnotePlugin'
import { HorizontalRulePlugin } from '../plugins/HorizontalRulePlugin'
import { ImagePlugin } from '../plugins/ImagePlugin'
import { KaTeXPlugin } from '../plugins/KaTeXPlugin'
import { MarkdownShortcutsPlugin } from '../plugins/MarkdownShortcutsPlugin'
import { MermaidPlugin } from '../plugins/MermaidPlugin'
import { OnChangePlugin } from '../plugins/OnChangePlugin'
import { SubmitShortcutPlugin } from '../plugins/SubmitShortcutPlugin'
import { editorTheme } from '../styles/theme'
import type { RichEditorProps } from '../types'
import { ContentEditable } from './ContentEditable'
import { clsx, getVariantClass } from './utils'

export function RichEditor({
  initialValue,
  onChange,
  variant = 'article',
  theme = 'light',
  placeholder = 'Write something...',
  onSubmit,
  autoFocus = false,
  className,
  contentClassName,
  style,
  actions,
  onEditorReady,
  extraNodes,
  rendererConfig,
  debounceMs,
  children,
}: RichEditorProps) {
  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allEditNodes, ...extraNodes] : allEditNodes
  setResolvedEditNodes(nodes)
  const initialConfig = {
    namespace: 'RichEditor',
    theme: editorTheme,
    nodes,
    editable: true,
    onError: (error: Error) => {
      console.error('[RichEditor]', error)
    },
    ...(initialValue ? { editorState: JSON.stringify(initialValue) } : {}),
  }

  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, theme)

  return (
    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass}>
      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
        <RendererConfigProvider
          config={rendererConfig}
          mode="editor"
          variant={variant}
        >
          <LexicalComposer initialConfig={initialConfig}>
            <FootnotePlugin>
              <div
                className={clsx('rich-editor', variantClass, className)}
                style={style}
                data-theme={theme}
              >
                <RichTextPlugin
                  contentEditable={
                    <ContentEditable
                      className={contentClassName}
                      placeholder={placeholder}
                    />
                  }
                  ErrorBoundary={LexicalErrorBoundary}
                />
                <HistoryPlugin />
                <ListPlugin />
                <LinkPlugin />
                <TabIndentationPlugin />
                <TablePlugin />
                <MarkdownShortcutsPlugin />
                <OnChangePlugin onChange={onChange} debounceMs={debounceMs} />
                <SubmitShortcutPlugin onSubmit={onSubmit} />
                <ImagePlugin />
                <KaTeXPlugin />
                <AlertPlugin />
                <MermaidPlugin />
                <HorizontalRulePlugin />
                <CheckListPlugin />
                <AutoLinkPlugin />
                <EditorRefPlugin onEditorReady={onEditorReady} />
                {autoFocus && <AutoFocusPlugin />}
                {children}
                {actions && (
                  <div className="rich-editor__actions">{actions}</div>
                )}
              </div>
            </FootnotePlugin>
          </LexicalComposer>
        </RendererConfigProvider>
      </ColorSchemeProvider>
    </PortalThemeProvider>
  )
}
```

```
import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
import { $nodesOfType } from 'lexical'
import { useCallback } from 'react'

import { useFootnoteDefinitions } from '../../context/FootnoteDefinitionsContext'
import { FootnoteSectionNode } from '../../nodes/FootnoteSectionNode'

export interface FootnoteSectionEditRendererProps {
  definitions: Record<string, string>
  nodeKey: string
}

export function FootnoteSectionEditRenderer({
  definitions,
}: FootnoteSectionEditRendererProps) {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
  const { displayNumberMap } = useFootnoteDefinitions()

  const sortedEntries = Object.entries(definitions).sort(
    ([a], [b]) => (displayNumberMap[a] ?? 0) - (displayNumberMap[b] ?? 0),
  )

  const handleContentChange = useCallback(
    (identifier: string, newContent: string) => {
      editor.update(() => {
        const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
        if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
          sectionNodes[0].setDefinition(identifier, newContent)
        }
      })
    },
    [editor],
  )

  const handleRemove = useCallback(
    (identifier: string) => {
      editor.update(() => {
        const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
        if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
          sectionNodes[0].removeDefinition(identifier)
        }
      })
    },
    [editor],
  )

  if (sortedEntries.length === 0) return null

  return (
    <div
      className="rich-footnote-section-content rich-footnote-section-edit"
      role="doc-endnotes"
    >
      <hr className="rich-footnote-section-divider" />
      <ol className="rich-footnote-section-list">
        {sortedEntries.map(([identifier, content]) => {
          const displayNum = displayNumberMap[identifier] ?? identifier
          return (
            <li
              key={identifier}
              id={`footnote-${identifier}`}
              className="rich-footnote-section-item rich-footnote-section-item-edit"
            >
              <span className="rich-footnote-section-item-num">
                {displayNum}.
              </span>
              <input
                type="text"
                className="rich-footnote-section-item-input"
                value={content}
                onChange={(e) =>
                  handleContentChange(identifier, e.target.value)
                }
                placeholder={`Footnote content for [^${identifier}]`}
              />
              <button
                type="button"
                className="rich-footnote-section-item-remove"
                onClick={() => handleRemove(identifier)}
                aria-label={`Remove footnote ${identifier}`}
              >
                ×
              </button>
            </li>
          )
        })}
      </ol>
    </div>
  )
}
```

```
import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
import { $getRoot, $nodesOfType } from 'lexical'
import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

import { FootnoteDefinitionsProvider } from '../context/FootnoteDefinitionsContext'
import { FootnoteNode } from '../nodes/FootnoteNode'
import {
  $createFootnoteSectionNode,
  FootnoteSectionNode,
} from '../nodes/FootnoteSectionNode'

export function FootnotePlugin({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
  const [definitions, setDefinitions] = useState<Record<string, string>>({})
  const [displayNumberMap, setDisplayNumberMap] = useState<
    Record<string, number>
  >({})
  const pendingUpdateRef = useRef(false)

  useEffect(() => {
    return editor.registerUpdateListener(({ editorState }) => {
      editorState.read(() => {
        const footnoteNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteNode)
        const seen = new Set<string>()
        const numberMap: Record<string, number> = {}
        let counter = 1
        for (const node of footnoteNodes) {
          const id = node.getIdentifier()
          if (!seen.has(id)) {
            seen.add(id)
            numberMap[id] = counter++
          }
        }
        setDisplayNumberMap(numberMap)

        const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)

        if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
          setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())
        } else {
          setDefinitions({})
        }

        if (!editor.isEditable() || pendingUpdateRef.current) return

        if (footnoteNodes.length === 0 && sectionNodes.length > 0) {
          pendingUpdateRef.current = true
          queueMicrotask(() => {
            editor.update(() => {
              const sections = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
              for (const s of sections) s.remove()
            })
            pendingUpdateRef.current = false
          })
          return
        }

        if (footnoteNodes.length > 0 && sectionNodes.length === 0) {
          const seenSnapshot = [...seen]
          pendingUpdateRef.current = true
          queueMicrotask(() => {
            editor.update(() => {
              const root = $getRoot()
              const defs: Record<string, string> = {}
              for (const id of seenSnapshot) {
                defs[id] = ''
              }
              const section = $createFootnoteSectionNode(defs)
              root.append(section)
            })
            pendingUpdateRef.current = false
          })
        } else if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
          const existingDefs = sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions()
          const missingIds = [...seen].filter((id) => !(id in existingDefs))
          const orphanIds = Object.keys(existingDefs).filter(
            (id) => !seen.has(id),
          )
          if (missingIds.length > 0 || orphanIds.length > 0) {
            pendingUpdateRef.current = true
            queueMicrotask(() => {
              editor.update(() => {
                const freshSections = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
                if (freshSections.length > 0) {
                  for (const id of missingIds) {
                    freshSections[0].setDefinition(id, '')
                  }
                  for (const id of orphanIds) {
                    freshSections[0].removeDefinition(id)
                  }
                }
              })
              pendingUpdateRef.current = false
            })
          }
        }
      })
    })
  }, [editor])

  return (
    <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
      definitions={definitions}
      displayNumberMap={displayNumberMap}
    >
      {children}
    </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
  )
}
```

```
import type {
  EditorConfig,
  LexicalEditor,
  LexicalNode,
  NodeKey,
  SerializedLexicalNode,
  Spread,
} from 'lexical'
import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
import type { ReactElement } from 'react'

import { FootnoteRenderer } from '../components/renderers/FootnoteRenderer'
import { createRendererDecoration } from '../components/RendererWrapper'

export type SerializedFootnoteNode = Spread<
  {
    identifier: string
  },
  SerializedLexicalNode
>

export class FootnoteNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
  __identifier: string

  static getType(): string {
    return 'footnote'
  }

  static clone(node: FootnoteNode): FootnoteNode {
    return new FootnoteNode(node.__identifier, node.__key)
  }

  constructor(identifier: string, key?: NodeKey) {
    super(key)
    this.__identifier = identifier
  }

  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    const sup = document.createElement('sup')
    sup.className = 'rich-footnote'
    return sup
  }

  updateDOM(): boolean {
    return false
  }

  isInline(): boolean {
    return true
  }

  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedFootnoteNode): FootnoteNode {
    return $createFootnoteNode(serializedNode.identifier)
  }

  exportJSON(): SerializedFootnoteNode {
    return {
      ...super.exportJSON(),
      type: 'footnote',
      identifier: this.__identifier,
      version: 1,
    }
  }

  getIdentifier(): string {
    return this.getLatest().__identifier
  }

  setIdentifier(identifier: string): void {
    const writable = this.getWritable()
    writable.__identifier = identifier
  }

  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    return createRendererDecoration('Footnote', FootnoteRenderer, {
      identifier: this.__identifier,
    })
  }
}

export function $createFootnoteNode(identifier: string): FootnoteNode {
  return new FootnoteNode(identifier)
}

export function $isFootnoteNode(
  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
): node is FootnoteNode {
  return node instanceof FootnoteNode
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```
import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
import { createContext, use, useMemo } from 'react'

export interface FootnoteDefinitionsContextValue {
  definitions: Record<string, string>
  displayNumberMap: Record<string, number>
}

const FootnoteDefinitionsContext =
  createContext<FootnoteDefinitionsContextValue>({
    definitions: {},
    displayNumberMap: {},
  })

export function FootnoteDefinitionsProvider({
  definitions,
  displayNumberMap,
  children,
}: FootnoteDefinitionsContextValue & { children: ReactNode }) {
  const value = useMemo(
    () => ({ definitions, displayNumberMap }),
    [definitions, displayNumberMap],
  )
  return (
    <FootnoteDefinitionsContext value={value}>
      {children}
    </FootnoteDefinitionsContext>
  )
}

export function useFootnoteDefinitions(): FootnoteDefinitionsContextValue {
  return use(FootnoteDefinitionsContext)
}

export function useFootnoteContent(identifier: string): string | undefined {
  const { definitions } = use(FootnoteDefinitionsContext)
  return definitions[identifier]
}

export function useFootnoteDisplayNumber(
  identifier: string,
): number | undefined {
  const { displayNumberMap } = use(FootnoteDefinitionsContext)
  return displayNumberMap[identifier]
}
```
</details></tool-use>

```
     8	  $createFootnoteSectionNode,
     9	  FootnoteSectionNode,
    10	} from '../nodes/FootnoteSectionNode'
    11	
    12	export function FootnotePlugin({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) {
    13	  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    14	  const [definitions, setDefinitions] = useState<Record<string, string>>({})
    15	  const [displayNumberMap, setDisplayNumberMap] = useState<
    16	    Record<string, number>
    17	  >({})
    18	  const pendingUpdateRef = useRef(false)
    19	
    20	  useEffect(() => {
    21	    return editor.registerUpdateListener(({ editorState }) => {
    22	      editorState.read(() => {
    23	        const footnoteNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteNode)
    24	        const seen = new Set<string>()
    25	        const numberMap: Record<string, number> = {}
    26	        let counter = 1
    27	        for (const node of footnoteNodes) {
    28	          const id = node.getIdentifier()
    29	          if (!seen.has(id)) {
    30	            seen.add(id)
    31	            numberMap[id] = counter++
    32	          }
    33	        }
    34	        setDisplayNumberMap(numberMap)
    35	
    36	        const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
    37	
    38	        if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
    39	          setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())
    40	        } else {
    41	          setDefinitions({})
    42	        }
    43	
    44	        if (!editor.isEditable() || pendingUpdateRef.current) return
    45	
    46	        if (footnoteNodes.length === 0 && sectionNodes.length > 0) {
    47	          pendingUpdateRef.current = true
    48	          queueMicrotask(() => {
    49	            editor.update(() => {
    50	              const sections = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
    51	              for (const s of sections) s.remove()
    52	            })
    53	            pendingUpdateRef.current = false
    54	          })
    55	          return
    56	        }
    57	
    58	        if (footnoteNodes.length > 0 && sectionNodes.length === 0) {
    59	          const seenSnapshot = [...seen]
    60	          pendingUpdateRef.current = true
    61	          queueMicrotask(() => {
    62	            editor.update(() => {
    63	              const root = $getRoot()
    64	              const defs: Record<string, string> = {}
    65	              for (const id of seenSnapshot) {
    66	                defs[id] = ''
    67	              }
    68	              const section = $createFootnoteSectionNode(defs)
    69	              root.append(section)
    70	            })
    71	            pendingUpdateRef.current = false
    72	          })
    73	        } else if (sectionNodes.length > 0) {
    74	          const existingDefs = sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions()
    75	          const missingIds = [...seen].filter((id) => !(id in existingDefs))
    76	          const orphanIds = Object.keys(existingDefs).filter(
    77	            (id) => !seen.has(id),
    78	          )
    79	          if (missingIds.length > 0 || orphanIds.length > 0) {
    80	            pendingUpdateRef.current = true
    81	            queueMicrotask(() => {
    82	              editor.update(() => {
    83	                const freshSections = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)
    84	                if (freshSections.length > 0) {
    85	                  for (const id of missingIds) {
    86	                    freshSections[0].setDefinition(id, '')
    87	                  }
    88	                  for (const id of orphanIds) {
    89	                    freshSections[0].removeDefinition(id)
    90	                  }
    91	                }
    92	              })
    93	              pendingUpdateRef.current = false
    94	            })
    95	          }
    96	        }
    97	      })
    98	    })
    99	  }, [editor])
   100	
   101	  return (
   102	    <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
   103	      definitions={definitions}
   104	      displayNumberMap={displayNumberMap}
   105	    >

   108	  )
   109	}
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 09:57:38Z)**_

- JSON deserialization will instantiate a `footnote-section` node via `FootnoteSectionNode.importJSON` and Lexical will therefore create/delete those nodes as it loads serialized editor state, so any saved definitions come back with the document; the Footnote plugin then watches the node graph and, whenever footnote markers exist without a section, queues `root.append($createFootnoteSectionNode(defs))`, meaning a section is always appended during the first post-import update when needed (`FootnoteSectionNode.ts:52-65`, `FootnotePlugin.tsx:58-72`).
- Both the base node and the edit node expose a `decorate()` that Lexical invokes whenever that node lives in the document; `FootnoteSectionEditNode.decorate` returns `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer`, so rendering the editable UI is conditional only on `sortedEntries` in that renderer, not on any external call (`FootnoteSectionEditNode.ts:20-31`, `FootnoteSectionNode.ts:94-103`, `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:19-47`).
- The bottom section stays hidden even though superscripts show because `FootnotePlugin` refuses to read `sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions()` whenever it is itself mutating the tree (`pendingUpdateRef` guard around `editor.update`): the listener returns early (`pendingUpdateRef.current` is true at `FootnotePlugin.tsx:44-74`), so the `definitions` state stays `{}`. `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer` sees that empty object and returns `null` (`FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:19-47`), so the renderer never produces DOM even though a section node exists.
- Next steps: 1) Ensure the plugin re-reads the new section’s definitions after the microtask insertion (for example, clear `pendingUpdateRef` before `setDefinitions` or dispatch a follow-up update) so `definitions` is no longer empty; 2) Verify that the renderer receives non-empty definitions after the first update by logging `displayNumberMap`/`definitions` immediately after the microtask-created section is appended.

---

The replacement object uses the Lexical 0.39 shape (`replace`, `with`, `withKlass`) so that any `FootnoteSectionNode` instance is swapped with a `FootnoteSectionEditNode` carrying the current definitions. This matches the documented pattern for Lexical 0.39’s node replacement API, so the syntax is valid.

2. **Edit configuration in RichEditor** – when the editor renders, it builds `nodes = extraNodes ? [...allEditNodes, ...extraNodes] : allEditNodes` and passes that into `LexicalComposer`’s `initialConfig` with `editable: true` (`packages/rich-editor/src/components/RichEditor.tsx:51-62`). Because `allEditNodes` spreads `customEditNodes` (which includes the replacement entry for `FootnoteSectionNode`), the edit-mode config that the user interacts with contains `FootnoteSectionEditNode`.

Without that replacement, the fallback would be `FootnoteSectionNode.importJSON` (`packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/FootnoteSectionNode.ts:52-65`), but the registered `withKlass` ensures the edit subclass is produced.

4. **decorate call and UI gating** – the actual node instance always runs its own `decorate()` (in this case `FootnoteSectionEditNode.decorate`, which creates `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer`; see `packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/FootnoteSectionEditNode.ts:26-31`). <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Detailing FootnotePlugin logic and likely render issue**

I’m explaining four key points clearly with line references: 

1) The definitions map isn’t directly built from footnote refs but is sourced from the first `FootnoteSectionNode` via `getDefinitions()` (lines 23–42). Footnote nodes are iterated and tracked in a `seen` set and `displayNumberMap` (lines 23–35), but definitions come from section node state (lines 36–42).

2) The plugin removes the footnote section if there are no footnote nodes but a section exists (lines 46–55), creates a new section with empty definitions if footnote nodes exist but no section node (lines 58–71), and patches definitions when there’s a mismatch (lines 73–95). It uses `pendingUpdateRef` and a microtask queue to avoid recursive updates (lines 44+).

3) Existing definitions from JSON can be dropped or overwritten when there are zero footnote nodes causing section removal (lines 46–55), and later a new section with empty definitions is appended (lines 58–71), effectively replacing any prior content.

4) The plugin relies on identifier strings as keys in the `seen` set and uses a numeric counter only for display ordering in `displayNumberMap` (lines 23–35). There’s no numeric normalization—strings like `"1"` and `"2"` are accepted as-is—so key mismatches won’t cause empty maps unless there are truly no footnote nodes.

The most likely cause for superscripts showing but the bottom footnote section hidden is this sequence: on update, the plugin sees zero footnote nodes and removes the section, resetting definitions to empty (lines 46–55). Shortly after, footnote nodes appear, and the plugin creates a new section node with empty string definitions for each footnote ID (lines 58–67). During this brief timing window, the renderer receives a definitions map with zero entries or only empty strings, causing it to render nothing (since it returns null if `sortedEntries.length === 0`) until a subsequent update populates definitions properly.

The user can verify the section node’s presence independently of the renderer by inspecting the editor state and querying for `FootnoteSectionNode` instances (lines 36–95).

This explanation references `packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx:23-95` for all behaviors described and highlights the asynchronous update timing as the root of the hidden section despite visible superscripts.
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:01:55Z)**_

- **Definitions flow (FootnotePlugin.tsx:23-42)** – the plugin never builds the definitions map directly from refs; it only inspects every `FootnoteNode` (lines 23‑35) to derive `seen` identifiers and the `displayNumberMap`. Immediately after those loops it reads `const sectionNodes = $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)` and, if any exist, calls `setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())` (lines 36‑40); otherwise it stores `{}`. So the actual data that drives the renderer always comes from the serialized section node stored in the tree, with the footnote nodes used only for numbering and determining when the section should be added/cleaned.

- **Creation/removal logic (FootnotePlugin.tsx:44‑95)** – the plugin removes every existing section when `footnoteNodes.length === 0 && sectionNodes.length > 0`, guarded by `pendingUpdateRef` and run inside a microtask (lines 46‑55). It creates a fresh one when footnotes exist but no section is present (`footnoteNodes.length > 0 && sectionNodes.length === 0`): it snapshots the seen IDs, queues an editor update that builds `defs[id] = ''` for each identifier, appends `$createFootnoteSectionNode(defs)` to the root, and then clears the guard (lines 58‑72). When both notes and a section exist, it compares the section’s definitions to `seen`, adding missing IDs (with empty strings) and removing orphaned ones (lines 73‑95).

- **Overwriting/dropping serialized definitions (FootnotePlugin.tsx:36‑72)** – if the serialized JSON contains a section but the initial update still sees zero `FootnoteNode`s (e.g., load order), the plugin immediately removes that section and calls `setDefinitions({})` before any renderer ever runs (lines 36‑55). When footnote refs later surface, it recreates the section with `defs[id] = ''` (lines 58‑67), discarding whatever textual content the serialized section previously held. That’s the path where definitions from JSON are effectively overwritten with an empty object even though refs exist at that point.

- **Identifier handling (FootnotePlugin.tsx:23‑33)** – identifiers are treated as strings: the plugin stores them in a `Set<string>` and uses them as keys in `numberMap`/`defs` (`lines 24‑33`). The only “numeric” aspect is the `counter` that produces a display order (`numberMap[id] = counter++`), but no coercion to/from actual numbers happens, so `'1'` vs `'2'` strings are preserved and cannot make the map empty by themselves.

- **Most likely root cause** – the plugin aggressively removes a section whenever it thinks no refs exist (lines 46‑55) and then re-adds one with blank definitions (lines 58‑67); during that removal/recreation window the renderer sees `definitions = {}` because the node was just replaced and has no entries, so `sortedEntries.length === 0` and `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer` returns `null` even though `FootnoteNode` superscripts are present. You can still observe the section node by inspecting `editorState.read(() => $nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode))` (the same helper used at lines 36‑38), so the missing UI is purely due to the empty `definitions` payload after the plugin’s removal/re-add cycle, not a missing node.

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:03:27Z)**_

- `config-edit.ts` exports `customEditNodes` as the same custom classes plus the `FootnoteSectionNode` entry followed immediately by its replacement object (`FootnoteNode`, `FootnoteSectionNode`, replacement config, then other nodes; see `customEditNodes` lines 21‑37). `allEditNodes` is literally `[...builtinNodes, ...customEditNodes]` (`config-edit.ts:45-48`), so Lexical receives the built-in rich-text/list/table/code nodes first, followed by every custom/edit node in the order shown. Because `FootnoteSectionNode` only appears once in `customEditNodes` before the replacement descriptor, there aren’t duplicate registrations—Lexical sees the node class once plus the replacement metadata, which is the intended pattern.

- The base `config.ts` shows `customNodes` containing `FootnoteSectionNode` as well (`config.ts:31-44`), but nothing else re-registers it, so `allEditNodes` (used for editing) simply inherits the same `FootnoteSectionNode` class once; the replacement entry injects `FootnoteSectionEditNode` behavior without duplicating the node type in Lexical’s node list. Thus the final `nodes` array passed to `LexicalComposer` (`RichEditor.tsx:51-62`) keeps the single `FootnoteSectionNode` registration plus the replacement config; no duplicate registrations should be causing the renderer suppression.

---

```
  registerCommand(command, listener, priority) {
    if (priority === undefined) {
      {
        formatDevErrorMessage(`Listener for type "command" requires a "priority".`);
      }
    }
    const commandsMap = this._commands;
    if (!commandsMap.has(command)) {
      commandsMap.set(command, [new Set(), new Set(), new Set(), new Set(), new Set()]);
    }
    const listenersInPriorityOrder = commandsMap.get(command);
    if (listenersInPriorityOrder === undefined) {
      {
        formatDevErrorMessage(`registerCommand: Command ${String(command)} not found in command map`);
      }
    }
    const listeners = listenersInPriorityOrder[priority];
    listeners.add(listener);
    return () => {
      listeners.delete(listener);
      if (listenersInPriorityOrder.every(listenersSet => listenersSet.size === 0)) {
        commandsMap.delete(command);
      }
    };
  }

  /**
   * Registers a listener that will run when a Lexical node of the provided class is
   * mutated. The listener will receive a list of nodes along with the type of mutation
   * that was performed on each: created, destroyed, or updated.
   *
   * One common use case for this is to attach DOM event listeners to the underlying DOM nodes as Lexical nodes are created.
   * {@link LexicalEditor.getElementByKey} can be used for this.

   * If any existing nodes are in the DOM, and skipInitialization is not true, the listener
   * will be called immediately with an updateTag of 'registerMutationListener' where all

        mutations.delete(listener);
      }

    }
    return registeredNode;
  }
```

```
function $parseSerializedNodeImpl(serializedNode, registeredNodes) {
  const type = serializedNode.type;
  const registeredNode = registeredNodes.get(type);
  if (registeredNode === undefined) {
    {
      formatDevErrorMessage(`parseEditorState: type "${type}" + not found`);
    }
  }
  const nodeClass = registeredNode.klass;
  if (serializedNode.type !== nodeClass.getType()) {
    {
      formatDevErrorMessage(`LexicalNode: Node ${nodeClass.name} does not implement .importJSON().`);
    }
  }
  const node = nodeClass.importJSON(serializedNode);
  const children = serializedNode.children;
  if ($isElementNode(node) && Array.isArray(children)) {
    for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
      const serializedJSONChildNode = children[i];
      const childNode = $parseSerializedNodeImpl(serializedJSONChildNode, registeredNodes);
      node.append(childNode);
    }
  }
  return node;
}
function parseEditorState(serializedEditorState, editor, updateFn) {
  const editorState = createEmptyEditorState();
  const previousActiveEditorState = activeEditorState;
  const previousReadOnlyMode = isReadOnlyMode;
  const previousActiveEditor = activeEditor;
  const previousDirtyElements = editor._dirtyElements;
  const previousDirtyLeaves = editor._dirtyLeaves;
  const previousCloneNotNeeded = editor._cloneNotNeeded;
  const previousDirtyType = editor._dirtyType;
  editor._dirtyElements = new Map();
  editor._dirtyLeaves = new Set();
  editor._cloneNotNeeded = new Set();
  editor._dirtyType = 0;
  activeEditorState = editorState;
  isReadOnlyMode = false;
  activeEditor = editor;
  setPendingNodeToClone(null);
  try {
    const registeredNodes = editor._nodes;
    const serializedNode = serializedEditorState.root;
    $parseSerializedNodeImpl(serializedNode, registeredNodes);
    if (updateFn) {
      updateFn();
    }

    // Make the editorState immutable
    editorState._readOnly = true;
    {
      handleDEVOnlyPendingUpdateGuarantees(editorState);
    }
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof Error) {
      editor._onError(error);
    }
  } finally {
    editor._dirtyElements = previousDirtyElements;
    editor._dirtyLeaves = previousDirtyLeaves;
    editor._cloneNotNeeded = previousCloneNotNeeded;
    editor._dirtyType = previousDirtyType;
    activeEditorState = previousActiveEditorState;
    isReadOnlyMode = previousReadOnlyMode;
    activeEditor = previousActiveEditor;
  }
  return editorState;
}
```

```
   * Registers a listener that will run when a Lexical node of the provided class is
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:06:40Z)**_

- `rom Lexical`’s parse path we see `parseEditorState` grab the registered node map and simply calls `registeredNode.klass.importJSON(serializedNode)` for every serialized entry (`packages/rich-editor/node_modules/lexical/Lexical.dev.mjs:8314-8348`). Replacement hooks like `replace`/`withKlass` are not in play there—only the node’s static `importJSON` runs.

- For the `'footnote-section'` type, that static method on `FootnoteSectionNode` immediately delegates to `$createFootnoteSectionNode(serializedNode.definitions)` (`packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/FootnoteSectionNode.ts:52-56`). `$createFootnoteSectionNode` then invokes `$applyNodeReplacement(new FootnoteSectionNode(...))` (`FootnoteSectionNode.ts:106-110`), which is the only place Lexical looks up the `replace`/`withKlass` config and actually instantiates the replacement.

- The edit config registers that replacement: `customEditNodes` lists `FootnoteSectionNode` and right afterward a replacement descriptor whose `replace` is the base node, `with` creates a `FootnoteSectionEditNode`, and `withKlass` names that class (`packages/rich-editor/src/config-edit.ts:21-37`). So whenever `$applyNodeReplacement` runs (including inside `importJSON`), it will swap in `FootnoteSectionEditNode`.

- **Conclusion:** even though `parseEditorState` only knows about the base class, the serialized `'footnote-section'` node is deserialized by `FootnoteSectionNode.importJSON`, which routes through `$applyNodeReplacement` and thus returns a `FootnoteSectionEditNode` instance. There is no separate “replacement after import” pass—import already yields the edit node because the base class’ loader explicitly applies the replacement configuration.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Analyzing pendingUpdateRef control flow and update skipping**

I’m mapping how `pendingUpdateRef` is set true and false (lines 18, 46-54, 58-72, 79-94) around queued microtasks for removal, creation, and update. The main insight is that while pending is true, update listeners return early (line 44), so updates triggered by section insertion (line 62) happen when pending is still true, causing definitions not to update. After pending resets false (line 71), no automatic update runs, leaving `definitions` stale and the bottom section missing despite footnote references showing. This explains the observed scenario where the section node exists but isn’t rendered due to an absent update after pending clears. The removal path (lines 46-55) only triggers when footnotes disappear, so it doesn't explain the symptom with existing footnotes. The timeline and line references clarify this deferred update behavior and its effect on the rendering state.
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:07:41Z)**_

- **pendingUpdateRef lifecycle** – defined with `useRef(false)` at lines 18‑19. Every queued tree mutation path flips it to `true` before scheduling the microtask (lines 46‑61, 80) and resets it to `false` once the microtask’s `editor.update` completes (lines 52‑54, 70‑72, 92‑94). The guard at line 44 (`if (!editor.isEditable() || pendingUpdateRef.current) return`) ensures any update listener runs concurrent with those queued mutations immediately bails out.

- **Skipped update path** – yes: when `footnoteNodes.length > 0 && sectionNodes.length === 0`, the plugin sets `pendingUpdateRef.current = true`, queues a microtask that appends a new section, and only after that mutation sets `pendingUpdateRef.current = false` (lines 58‑72). The `editor.update` inside the microtask adds the section and triggers another update listener run, but that run still sees `pendingUpdateRef.current === true` (because it hasn’t been reset yet) and returns at line 44 without reading `sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions()` or updating `definitions`. Once the microtask finishes, `pendingUpdateRef` becomes `false`, but no automatic update follows, so `definitions` remains `{}` until the user makes another edit. During that idle period the renderer sees an empty `definitions` prop and returns `null`, even though the section node exists.

- **Symptom timeline** – when serialized state or user hover introduces a new footnote reference, FootnotePlugin goes down the creation path (lines 58‑72). Before the new section’s insertion can be read into `definitions`, the listener has already exited due to `pendingUpdateRef` (lines 44‑45). After the microtask’s `editor.update` runs (lines 62‑69), the very next listener fire also exits early (line 44) because `pendingUpdateRef` is still `true` until line 71. No additional update happens until the next keystroke, so `definitions` stays empty (`lines 36‑42`), the renderer returns `null` (`FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:47`), and the bottom section stays hidden while the superscript markers still render.

- **Section removal/recreation** – there’s no evidence from this control flow that the section is removed and never recreated when footnotes exist: removal only happens when `footnoteNodes.length === 0` (lines 46‑55), so the current symptom is instead explained by the “missing update after creation” path above rather than a permanent deletion.

2. **`FootnoteSectionEditNode.ts` (decorate returns renderer props):**


(`packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx`: lines 36‑42)

**Conclusion:** The renderer renders based on the `definitions` prop passed directly from the node’s `decorate()` call, not the plugin state. The plugin’s `setDefinitions` merely mirrors whatever is already stored inside the `FootnoteSectionNode`; it does not feed the renderer. If the renderer receives an empty `definitions` prop, that means the section node itself holds no entries at render time.

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:08:51Z)**_

- The renderer shows every entry in `sortedEntries` regardless of whether its text is empty because it just maps each `[identifier, content]` pair to an `<li>` with an `<input value={content}>`. There is no filtering condition; even definitions with empty strings create inputs (`FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:47-84`). This means the renderer only returns `null` when `sortedEntries.length === 0`.

- `sortedEntries` itself is computed from the `definitions` prop passed into the renderer (`FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:13-21`). Empty-definition entries remain in the map, so an empty section node (no keys) is the only way to make the renderer exit early.

- The plugin-maintained state (`setDefinitions`) is only used elsewhere and does not gate rendering; the renderer always renders whatever the node supplies in its own `definitions` prop.

```
import type { MouseEvent } from 'react'
import { useCallback } from 'react'

import { useFootnoteDefinitions } from '../../context/FootnoteDefinitionsContext'

export interface FootnoteSectionRendererProps {
  definitions: Record<string, string>
  nodeKey: string
}

export function FootnoteSectionRenderer({
  definitions,
}: FootnoteSectionRendererProps) {
  const { displayNumberMap } = useFootnoteDefinitions()

  const sortedEntries = Object.entries(definitions).sort(
    ([a], [b]) => (displayNumberMap[a] ?? 0) - (displayNumberMap[b] ?? 0),
  )

  if (sortedEntries.length === 0) return null

  return (
    <div className="rich-footnote-section-content" role="doc-endnotes">
      <hr className="rich-footnote-section-divider" />
      <ol className="rich-footnote-section-list">
        {sortedEntries.map(([identifier, content]) => {
          const displayNum = displayNumberMap[identifier] ?? identifier
          return (
            <FootnoteSectionItem
              key={identifier}
              identifier={identifier}
              content={content}
              displayNum={displayNum}
            />
          )
        })}
      </ol>
    </div>
  )
}

function FootnoteSectionItem({
  identifier,
  content,
  displayNum,
}: {
  identifier: string
  content: string
  displayNum: number | string
}) {
  const targetId = `footnote-${identifier}`
  const refId = `footnote-ref-${identifier}`

  const handleBackClick = useCallback(
    (e: MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>) => {
      e.preventDefault()
      const refElement = document.getElementById(refId)
      if (!refElement) return
      refElement.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' })
      refElement.classList.add('rich-footnote-highlight')
      window.setTimeout(() => {
        refElement.classList.remove('rich-footnote-highlight')
      }, 1200)
    },
    [refId],
  )

  return (
    <li
      id={targetId}
      className="rich-footnote-section-item"
      value={typeof displayNum === 'number' ? displayNum : undefined}
    >
      <span className="rich-footnote-section-item-content">{content}</span>
      <a
        href={`#${refId}`}
        onClick={handleBackClick}
        className="rich-footnote-back-ref"
        role="doc-backlink"
        aria-label={`Back to reference ${displayNum}`}
      >
        ↩
      </a>
    </li>
  )
}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:11:03Z)**_

**FootnotePlugin.tsx flow (lines refer to `packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/FootnotePlugin.tsx`):**
1. Initialization (lines 12‑19): grab editor context, init `definitions`/`displayNumberMap` state, and `pendingUpdateRef = false`.
2. Update listener registration (20‑99): on every update:
   - Read all `FootnoteNode`s (`lines 23‑33`) to build `seen` set + `numberMap`.
   - Update `displayNumberMap` with `numberMap` (`line 34`).
   - Grab existing `FootnoteSectionNode`s (`line 36`).
   - If a section exists, `setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())` else `setDefinitions({})` (`36‑42`).
3. Early exit guard (44): return immediately whenever the editor is read-only or `pendingUpdateRef.current` is true.
4. Removal branch (46‑55): if no footnotes but a section exists, queue a microtask that removes all section nodes; during that microtask `pendingUpdateRef` is true so subsequent listener invocations skip.
5. Creation branch (58‑72): if footnotes exist but no section, snapshot `seen`, set `pendingUpdateRef = true`, queue microtask that:
   - Builds empty definitions for each seen ID (`64‑67`).
   - Creates/appends a new section node via `$createFootnoteSectionNode(defs)` (`68‑69`).
   - After the microtask completes, clears `pendingUpdateRef`.
   - Note: no immediate `setDefinitions` occurs while `pendingUpdateRef` is true, so the renderer sees `{}` until another update fires after the microtask.
6. Sync branch (73‑95): when both footnotes and a section exist, compute `missingIds`/`orphanIds` (`74‑77`). If there are discrepancies, queue microtask (with `pendingUpdateRef` toggled) that adds/removes definitions on the fresh section node (`80‑94`).

**Empty/hidden section trigger:** the creation branch (lines 58‑72) is the culprit. After the section is appended, the very next listener run still sees `pendingUpdateRef.current === true` and exits at line 44 before `setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())` can populate state. Because no more updates happen until user input, `definitions` stays `{}`, so `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer` returns `null` even though the section node now exists.

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:12:18Z)**_

- **Definitions source:** `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer` only uses the `definitions` prop passed from the node; it sorts `Object.entries(definitions)` and renders every entry (empty or not) — no plugin context values are used for the list itself (`FootnoteSectionEditRenderer.tsx:8-84`). `FootnoteSectionEditNode.decorate()` sends `{ definitions: this.__definitions }` to the renderer, so the node’s payload dictates visibility (`FootnoteSectionEditNode.ts:26-31`).

- **Plugin mirror:** `FootnotePlugin` just tracks and mirrors whatever definitions live on the section node: it reads them from `$nodesOfType(FootnoteSectionNode)` and calls `setDefinitions(sectionNodes[0].getDefinitions())` (or `{}` when no section exists) at every update (`FootnotePlugin.tsx:36-42`). It never injects a different object into the renderer.

- **sync branch detail:** When footnotes and a section exist, the plugin computes `missingIds`/`orphanIds` from the `seen` set and existing definitions, and queues an update that adds/removes keys so the node stays in sync (`FootnotePlugin.tsx:73-95`). No definitions are dropped unless the section node itself is empty/removed.

- **Section creation bug:** The creation branch (`footnoteNodes.length > 0 && sectionNodes.length === 0`) sets `pendingUpdateRef` true, appends a new section with empty strings for every footnote ID, and only clears the flag after the microtask. Every listener invocation during that window returns early (`FootnotePlugin.tsx:58-72` and the guard at `:44`), so `definitions` remains `{}` the rest of that event loop even though the section exists. The renderer sees zero entries and returns `null`, which explains why superscripts show but the bottom section stays hidden until another update runs.

- **Outcome:** There’s no automatic follow-up read after the microtask-created section, so the plugin never re-populates `definitions`. To fix the hidden section, re-trigger `setDefinitions` (or allow the update listener to continue) immediately after the microtask finishes, ensuring the renderer sees the freshly appended section’s definitions before returning `null`.

```
import type { ComponentType, ReactElement } from 'react'
import { createElement } from 'react'

import { useRendererConfig } from '../context/RendererConfigContext'
import type { RendererConfig } from '../types/renderer-config'

export type RendererKey = keyof RendererConfig

type RendererPropsByKey = {
  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]> extends ComponentType<
    infer P extends object
  >
    ? P
    : never
}

type RendererComponentByKey = {
  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<
    RendererConfig[K]
  > extends ComponentType<any>
    ? NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]>
    : never
}

export type RendererWrapperProps = {
  [K in RendererKey]-?: {
    /** Name of the renderer in RendererConfig */
    rendererKey: K
    /** Default renderer component */
    defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K]
    /** Props to pass to the renderer */
    props: RendererPropsByKey[K]
  }
}[RendererKey]

/**
 * Wrapper component that allows overriding default renderers with custom ones.
 * Uses RendererConfig from context to determine which renderer to use.
 */
export function RendererWrapper({
  rendererKey,
  defaultRenderer: DefaultRenderer,
  props,
}: RendererWrapperProps) {
  const config = useRendererConfig()
  const Renderer: ComponentType<any> = config?.[rendererKey] ?? DefaultRenderer

  return <Renderer {...props} />
}

/**
 * Type-safe helper for creating RendererWrapper elements from .ts node files.
 * Avoids the createElement + discriminated-union typing limitation.
 */
export function createRendererDecoration<K extends RendererKey>(
  rendererKey: K,
  defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K],
  props: RendererPropsByKey[K],
): ReactElement {
  return createElement(RendererWrapper, {
    rendererKey,
    defaultRenderer,
    props,
  } as RendererWrapperProps)
}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.1-codex-mini 2026-02-25 10:14:20Z)**_

- `createRendererDecoration` lives in `packages/rich-editor/src/components/RendererWrapper.tsx` and always returns a `RendererWrapper` ReactElement; it just passes the requested `rendererKey`, default component, and props along (`RendererWrapper.tsx`: lines 15‑45). There’s no conditional `null` return for editable mode or missing renderers—`RendererWrapper` simply picks the override from `RendererConfig` if present, otherwise `defaultRenderer` (`RendererWrapper.tsx`: lines 34‑42).

- Because `FootnoteSectionNode.decorate()` always supplies the same `rendererKey` and `definitions` payload, the base node won’t silently disappear in edit mode; the node always produces a decorator as long as `createRendererDecoration` completes without throwing (`FootnoteSectionNode.ts:94‑102`). The edit subclass `FootnoteSectionEditNode` bypasses `RendererWrapper` entirely and renders `FootnoteSectionEditRenderer` directly, so the only scenario where the section is hidden even though the node exists is when the node’s `definitions` object is empty (the renderer returns `null` when `sortedEntries.length === 0`).
